quiz_questions.question_text: A wheel rotates at 600 revolutions per minute. What is its angular velocity in rad/s?
quiz_questions.explanation: ω = 600 rev/min × (2π rad/rev) × (1 min/60 s) = 600(2π)/60 = 20π rad/s ≈ 62.8 rad/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: A disk starts from rest and reaches an angular velocity of 10 rad/s in 5 seconds with constant angular acceleration. What is the angular acceleration?
quiz_questions.explanation: α = Δω/Δt = (10 - 0)/5 = 2 rad/s².

quiz_questions.question_text: A point on the rim of a wheel of radius 0.5 m rotates at ω = 4 rad/s. What is its tangential speed?
A wheel undergoes constant angular acceleration of 3 rad/s² from rest. How many radians does it rotate in 4 seconds?
quiz_questions.explanation: θ = ω₀t + ½αt² = 0 + ½(3)(16) = 24 rad.

quiz_questions.question_text: A flywheel spinning at 30 rad/s decelerates uniformly at 6 rad/s². How many revolutions does it make before stopping?
quiz_questions.explanation: ω² = ω₀² + 2αθ: 0 = 900 - 12θ, θ = 75 rad. Revolutions = 75/(2π) ≈ 11.9 rev.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the centripetal acceleration of a point 0.2 m from the center of a disk rotating at 10 rad/s?
A merry-go-round has angular velocity ω(t) = 2t - 0.5t² rad/s. What is the angular acceleration at t = 2 s?
quiz_questions.explanation: α(t) = dω/dt = 2 - t. At t = 2 s: α = 2 - 2 = 0 rad/s².

quiz_questions.question_text: A bicycle wheel of radius 0.35 m has a tangential acceleration of 1.4 m/s² at its rim. What is the angular acceleration?
quiz_questions.explanation: a_t = rα, so α = a_t/r = 1.4/0.35 = 4 rad/s².

quiz_questions.question_text: Two gears mesh together. Gear A has 20 teeth and Gear B has 40 teeth. If Gear A rotates at 100 rpm, what is Gear B's angular velocity?
quiz_questions.explanation: For meshing gears: N_A × ω_A = N_B × ω_B. 20(100) = 40(ω_B), ω_B = 50 rpm.

quiz_questions.question_text: A wheel of radius 0.3 m rolls without slipping at 5 m/s. What is its angular velocity?
quiz_questions.explanation: For rolling without slipping: v = rω, so ω = v/r = 5/0.3 = 16.67 rad/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is angular velocity?

options.text: The linear speed of a rotating object
The rate of change of angular position: ω = dθ/dt, measured in rad/s

quiz_questions.explanation: Angular velocity ω = dθ/dt is the rate of change of angular position with time, measured in radians per second.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is angular acceleration?

options.text: The centripetal acceleration of a rotating point
The rate of change of angular velocity: α = dω/dt, measured in rad/s²
The tangential speed divided by the radius
The torque divided by the moment of inertia

quiz_questions.explanation: Angular acceleration α = dω/dt = d²θ/dt² is the rate at which angular velocity changes, measured in rad/s².

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'tangential acceleration' mean for a point on a rotating body?

options.text: The component of acceleration directed toward the center of rotation
The total acceleration of the point
The component of acceleration tangent to the circular path, equal to rα

quiz_questions.explanation: Tangential acceleration a_t = rα is the component along the direction of motion, changing the speed of the point. It is zero when ω is constant.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'rolling without slipping' mean?

options.text: The contact point has zero velocity relative to the surface, giving the constraint v = rω
The object rolls with no friction at all
The object slides and rolls simultaneously

quiz_questions.explanation: Rolling without slipping means the contact point is instantaneously at rest relative to the surface. This gives the constraint v_cm = rω, linking translation and rotation.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the relationship between linear and angular quantities in rotational motion?

options.text: v = rω, a_t = rα, and a_c = rω²
Linear and angular quantities are independent

quiz_questions.explanation: The key relationships are: tangential velocity v = rω, tangential acceleration a_t = rα, and centripetal acceleration a_c = v²/r = rω².

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a radian?

options.text: The angle that corresponds to one revolution
The angle subtended at the center of a circle by an arc equal in length to the radius

quiz_questions.explanation: A radian is the angle subtended when the arc length equals the radius: θ = s/r. One full circle = 2π radians ≈ 6.28 rad.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the period of rotation?

options.text: The time for one complete revolution: T = 2π/ω
The number of revolutions per second
The angular displacement per unit time

quiz_questions.explanation: The period T is the time for one full revolution. T = 2π/ω, where ω is the angular velocity. Frequency f = 1/T = ω/(2π).

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the difference between angular velocity and angular speed?

options.text: Angular velocity is measured in rpm while angular speed is in rad/s
Angular velocity is a vector quantity (with direction along the axis of rotation) while angular speed is its scalar magnitude
Angular speed includes direction but angular velocity does not

quiz_questions.explanation: Angular velocity ω is a vector along the rotation axis (direction by right-hand rule). Angular speed |ω| is the scalar magnitude, just how fast the rotation occurs.

quiz_questions.question_text: What are the rotational kinematic equations?

options.text: Equations relating angular position, angular velocity, angular acceleration, and time for constant angular acceleration
Equations relating torque and angular momentum
Equations that only apply to objects rolling without slipping

quiz_questions.explanation: The rotational kinematic equations (ω = ω₀ + αt, θ = ω₀t + ½αt², ω² = ω₀² + 2αθ) are analogous to the linear kinematic equations for constant angular acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'centripetal acceleration' mean in rotational motion?

options.text: Acceleration that causes an object to spiral outward
Acceleration tangent to the circular path
Acceleration directed toward the center of the circular path: a_c = v²/r = rω²
The rate at which angular velocity changes

quiz_questions.explanation: Centripetal (center-seeking) acceleration points radially inward and is responsible for changing the direction of velocity: a_c = v²/r = rω².

quiz_questions.question_text: A car tire (radius 0.3 m) accelerates from rest to 80 km/h in 10 seconds. What is the angular acceleration of the tire, and how many revolutions does it make during this acceleration?
A centrifuge spins biological samples at 12,000 rpm. If the samples are 0.1 m from the axis, what is the centripetal acceleration in terms of g? (g = 9.8 m/s²)

A hard drive disk spins at 7200 rpm. When the computer shuts down, it takes 8 seconds for the disk to stop. What angular deceleration does the disk experience, and how many revolutions occur during spin-down?
A satellite orbits Earth at an altitude where the orbital period is 90 minutes. What is the angular velocity, and if the orbital radius is 6,671 km, what is the satellite's linear speed?
A vinyl record player must bring the record from rest to 33⅓ rpm in exactly 0.5 revolutions. What constant angular acceleration is needed?
quiz_questions.explanation: ω_f = 33.33(2π/60) = 3.49 rad/s. θ = 0.5(2π) = π rad. Using ω² = 2αθ: (3.49)² = 2α(π). α = 12.18/(2π) ≈ 1.94 rad/s². Actually 12.18/6.28 ≈ 1.94. But the answer says 3.88, so let me recheck: ω² = ω₀² + 2αθ. 12.18 = 2α(3.14). α = 12.18/6.28 = 1.94. The closest is 1.94.

quiz_questions.question_text: A wind turbine blade is 50 m long and rotates at 15 rpm. What is the speed of the blade tip, and what centripetal acceleration does the tip experience?
An electric motor's angular velocity follows ω(t) = 100(1 - e^(-t/2)) rad/s. What is the angular acceleration at t = 0 s and at t = 4 s?
A potter's wheel (radius 0.25 m) spins at 3 rev/s. A piece of clay at the rim would fly off if the centripetal acceleration exceeds 50 m/s². Will the clay stay on?

options.text: Yes, a_c ≈ 44.4 m/s² is below 50 m/s²
Yes, a_c ≈ 22.2 m/s² is below 50 m/s²

quiz_questions.explanation: ω = 3(2π) = 6π rad/s ≈ 18.85 rad/s. a_c = rω² = 0.25(18.85)² = 0.25(355.3) ≈ 88.8 m/s². This exceeds 50 m/s², so the clay flies off.

quiz_questions.question_text: A DVD starts at rest and reaches its playing speed of 570 rpm with a constant angular acceleration of 50 rad/s². How long does this take, and what angle does it rotate through?
A Ferris wheel of radius 20 m completes one revolution in 40 seconds. A rider at the top has what total acceleration (both centripetal and tangential) if the wheel rotates at constant speed?

options.text: Approximately 0.49 m/s² directed toward the center
Approximately 3.14 m/s² directed toward the center

quiz_questions.explanation: ω = 2π/40 = π/20 rad/s. At constant speed, tangential acceleration = 0. a_c = rω² = 20(π/20)² = 20(π²/400) = π²/20 ≈ 0.49 m/s² toward center.
